description.task: Implement the Repository pattern for a Product entity. The repository abstracts data access operations (CRUD) behind an interface. Implement an in-memory version. Demonstrate how Spring Data JPA's JpaRepository models this pattern.
instructions: save() uses the product's id as the map key, overwriting any existing product with the same id
findById() wraps the map lookup in Optional.ofNullable()
findByCategory() filters products case-insensitively: use equalsIgnoreCase() on the category field
findByMaxPrice() filters products where price <= maxPrice and sorts by price ascending
delete() removes the product by id; no-op if id not found
description.hint: findByCategory(): return store.values().stream().filter(p -> p.category().equalsIgnoreCase(category)).collect(...). findByMaxPrice(): filter p.price() <= maxPrice then sort with Comparator.comparingDouble(Product::price).
description.expectedOutput:
Count: 3
Find 2: Mouse
Electronics: 2
After delete: 2
Cheap items: [Mouse, Desk]

title: Template Method Pattern for Report Generation

description.task: Implement the Template Method pattern for generating reports. An abstract ReportGenerator defines the fixed algorithm steps (fetchData → processData → formatOutput → export). Subclasses override specific steps for CSV and JSON report formats.
instructions: The template method generate() is final and must not be overridden
CsvReportGenerator.formatOutput() joins each row's elements with ',' and rows with newlines
JsonReportGenerator.formatOutput() formats as '[{"name":"Alice"},{"age":"30"}]' (each row as key-value JSON object)
Both subclasses override export() to prefix the content with 'CSV:\n' and 'JSON:\n' respectively
processData() is a hook with a default identity implementation; subclasses may optionally override it
description.hint: JsonReportGenerator.formatOutput(): build a StringBuilder, iterate rows, format as '{"' + row[0] + '":"' + row[1] + '"}', join with ',' inside brackets '[...]'.

description.task: Implement the Data Transfer Object (DTO) pattern with a Mapper for a User entity. The entity contains sensitive fields (password hash, internal id). The DTO exposes only safe fields for API responses. The mapper converts between entity and DTO.
instructions: toDto() creates a UserDto from entity, copying only username, email, and role fields
toAdminDto() creates an AdminUserDto including id, username, email, role, and active (excluding passwordHash)
toDtoList() maps each entity in the list to a UserDto using toDto()
Neither DTO should ever include the passwordHash field
toDtoList() preserves the original order of the input list
description.hint: toDto(): return new UserDto(entity.username(), entity.email(), entity.role()). toAdminDto(): return new AdminUserDto(entity.id(), entity.username(), entity.email(), entity.role(), entity.active()). toDtoList(): stream map toDto.
description.expectedOutput:
DTO username: alice
DTO email: alice@example.com
Admin ID: 42
Admin active: true
DTO list size: 2
